name: JBrowse Navigator
description: Learn to navigate a genome browser through guided exploration

awards.name: First Steps
awards.flavorText: You moved! The genome stretches out in both directions. What's out there?

awards.name: Eagle Eye
awards.flavorText: Individual nucleotides. You're reading the source code of a living thing.

awards.name: Curator
awards.flavorText: A new layer of evidence, revealed.

awards.name: Collector
awards.flavorText: Multiple data types, one view. That's how real analysis works.

awards.name: Biologist
awards.flavorText: You speak the language of genomes.

tasks.title: Change Your View
tasks.description: Try zooming the genome view. Use the + and - buttons in the header bar above the genome display.
hints: Look for the + and - zoom buttons in the genome view header

coaching.message: Try the + and - buttons in the genome view header

tasks.title: Look Around
tasks.description: Click and drag the genome view to slide left or right along the chromosome.
hints: Click and hold on the genome display, then drag sideways

coaching.message: Click and hold on the genome display, then drag left or right

tasks.title: Read the Code
tasks.description: Make sure the reference sequence track is visible (open the track selector if needed — look for 'volvox' under 'Reference sequence'). Then zoom in with the + button until you see individual colored letters (A, T, G, C).
hints: First: open the track selector and make sure the reference sequence track is turned on.
Then zoom in with the + button. The letters appear at the highest zoom levels.

tasks.title: Reveal Hidden Data
tasks.description: The genome has more data than what's visible. Open the track selector (look for 'Open track selector' text or a '+' icon at the bottom-left of the genome view) and turn on the 'GFF3Tabix genes' track.
hints: Look below the genome view for a button or text that says 'Open track selector'
In the track list, search for 'GFF3Tabix' or look under gene annotations

coaching.message: Look for 'Open track selector' at the bottom-left of the genome view

tasks.title: Name That Gene
tasks.description: With the gene track visible, zoom in to a comfortable level where you can read feature labels (try around 1-10 bp per pixel). You should see colored blocks with names. What's the name of any gene you can see?
hints: Zoom in until you see text labels on the colored blocks
Gene names in this genome include EDEN, Apple1, Apple2, seg01, etc.
If you don't see labels, zoom in more — they appear at higher magnification

tasks.title: The Other Contig
tasks.description: This genome has two contigs (chromosome-like sequences): ctgA (~50kb) and ctgB (~6kb). Navigate to ctgB. You can type 'ctgB' in the search bar at the top of the genome view.
hints: The search bar is at the top of the genome view — type 'ctgB' and press Enter
ctgB is much shorter than ctgA

tasks.title: Gene Anatomy
tasks.description: Navigate to ctgA and find the gene called 'EDEN' (try searching for it). Zoom in to see its internal structure. The thick colored blocks are exons (coding regions). How many exons does EDEN have?
hints: Type 'EDEN' in the search bar to jump to it
Exons are the thick blocks; thin lines between them are introns
Count each distinct thick block

tasks.title: The Neighbor
tasks.description: Starting at gene EDEN on ctgA, pan to the right (click-drag). What is the name of the next gene you find?
hints: Navigate to EDEN first (search for it), then click-drag rightward
Zoom in if you need to read the label

tasks.title: Variant Data
tasks.description: Open the track selector and add the 'volvox filtered vcf' track. This shows genetic variants — places where this genome differs from a reference.
hints: Open the track selector and search for 'filtered vcf'
The track shows small colored markers at variant positions

tasks.title: Quick Check
tasks.description: What are the two contigs in this genome?

tasks.title: Layers of Evidence
tasks.description: Open at least 3 tracks simultaneously (e.g., genes, variants, and an alignments or coverage track). Find a region where the different data types overlap interestingly. Describe what you see in 1-2 sentences.
hints: Try adding a coverage or alignments track alongside genes and variants
Look for regions where variant calls overlap with gene features
Zoom in to see individual reads in the alignments track

tasks.title: Free Exploration
tasks.description: Spend a minute freely exploring. Navigate anywhere, open any tracks, zoom to different scales. When done, describe the most interesting thing you found.
hints: Try zooming to different levels: overview, gene-level, base-pair level
Open tracks you haven't tried yet
Look for patterns: coverage changes, variant clusters, gene-dense regions

tasks.title: Teach a Robot
tasks.description: Navigate to a region you find interesting. Describe step-by-step how you got there and what makes it worth looking at. Your description will help train an AI navigation agent.
hints: Think about what actions you took: zoom, pan, open tracks
Explain why you chose to look where you did

tasks.title: Graduation
tasks.description: Final question: what would you want to explore further in this genome, or in a larger genome, if you had more time?
